hero-title: Let's start at the very beginning
Begin right here with the basics
hero-lede: You don't need to know anything yet, and there's nothing you missed. These are five short, easy reads. They tell you what AI really is, the things it's good at, the times it gets things wrong, and what could earn your trust — and what might not. Everything is written in simple, everyday words.

You need no experience, and there's nothing to catch up on. These five short reads explain what AI really is, where it's good, where it goes wrong, and what could earn your trust — all in plain, friendly words.
No experience needed, and nothing to catch up on. These five short reads explain what AI really is, where it shines, where it slips up, and what could earn your trust — all in plain words.
No experience required. Five short reads cover what AI is, where it shines, where it slips, and what could earn your trust — plainly put.
Five short reads: what AI is, its strengths, its failures, and what could earn your trust.
card-what-is-ai: Here is the question behind everything, and one you get to answer for yourself: is AI here to help you, or to take your place? There's also a small game where you decide who should do a task — you, or AI.
The question at the heart of it, and one you get to answer yourself: is AI here to help you, or to replace you? There's also a small interactive bit where you decide who should do a job — you, or AI.

The question this whole site turns on — and one you get to answer yourself: is AI here to help you, or to replace you? Plus a little interactive bit where you weigh up who should do a job — you, or AI.
The core question: does AI help you, or replace you? You decide. Includes a short interactive task — you pick who should do the job, you or AI.
Core question: does AI assist, or replace? You decide — plus an interactive who-does-the-job choice.
card-why-need-it: How working with AI compares with the two other choices — ignoring it, or letting it do everything for you — and whether right now is a good time to start feeling comfortable with it. You weigh it up.
How working with AI stacks up against ignoring it or handing it everything — and whether now is a good time to get comfortable with it.
How working with AI stacks up against ignoring it or handing everything over — and whether now's a good moment to get comfortable.
How partnering with AI compares with ignoring it or fully delegating — and whether now's the moment to get comfortable.
How working with AI compares with ignoring or over-delegating — and whether now.
card-how-it-helps: The three simple, real-life ways AI makes your day easier: it does things faster, it takes over the dull jobs, and it helps you think problems through. We show you real examples of each one.
The three down-to-earth ways AI makes your day easier — doing things faster, taking the boring jobs, and helping you think things through — with real examples.
The three down-to-earth ways AI lightens your day — speeding things up, taking the boring jobs, and helping you think things through — with real examples.
Three practical ways AI lightens your day — speed, dull tasks, and clearer thinking — with real examples.
Three ways AI helps: speed, drudgery, thinking — with examples.
card-pitfalls: Now the honest part: AI can sound very sure of itself and still be wrong. This read shows you the places where it usually gets things wrong, so you always know when it's worth checking again.
The honest part: AI can sound sure and still be wrong. Here's where it usually trips up, so you always know when to double-check.
The honest bit: AI can sound sure and still be wrong. Here's where it tends to trip, so you always know when to double-check.
The honest part: AI can sound confident and still be wrong. Here's where it trips, so you know when to verify.
AI can be confidently wrong. Where it fails, and when to verify.
card-three-pillars: Three things some say change AI from a clever guesser into something you can really count on. Look at them and judge for yourself — and see whether they help you tell good AI apart from shaky AI.
Three things some say turn a clever guesser into something you can really rely on. See whether they hold up — and whether they help you tell good AI from shaky AI.
Three things some say turn a clever guesser into something you can rely on. See if they hold up — and whether they help you tell good AI from shaky AI.
Three things said to turn a clever guesser into something reliable — see if they hold, and whether they help you tell good AI from shaky AI.
Three things said to make AI reliable, not just clever — judge for yourself.

next-body: Got the basics now? Lovely. Next are the handy mental models that quietly make you better at all this. Or you can drop into your review queue to help whatever's started to click really stick. There's no rush — go whichever way feels right.
